chunk_summary: Prophylactic iron-folic acid (IFA) schedules under Anemia Mukt Bharat target children, adolescents, and pregnant women through health and education platforms, emphasizing lifecycle anemia prevention and supply chain alignment.
learning_objectives: Understand prophylactic IFA schedules for different age groups under Anemia Mukt Bharat.
Learn delivery platforms for IFA supplementation, including schools, anganwadi centers, and ASHA outreach.
Recognize the importance of aligning prescriptions with state supply chain provisions.
Integrate anemia prevention with deworming, dietary counseling, and follow-up hemoglobin checks.
key_facts: Children 6–59 months: Biweekly IFA syrup (20 mg elemental iron + 100 µg folic acid).
School-age children 5–10 years: Weekly IFA tablet (45 mg elemental iron + 400 µg folic acid).
Adolescents 10–19 years: Weekly IFA tablet (60 mg elemental iron + 500 µg folic acid).
Pregnant and lactating women: Daily IFA prophylaxis recommended.
Older programs like WIFS used tablets with 100 mg elemental iron + 500 µg folic acid.
Prophylaxis should be linked with deworming, dietary counseling, and follow-up hemoglobin checks.
reference_quote: AMB operationalizes anemia prevention as a lifecycle strategy delivered through both health and education platforms.
document_key_topics: Prophylactic IFA schedules for children, adolescents, and pregnant women.
Delivery platforms for IFA supplementation (schools, anganwadi, ASHA outreach).
Integration of anemia prevention with deworming and dietary counseling.
